Container.Name: HELBACKUP
Container.Overview: HELBACKUP is an intelligent backup orchestrator specifically designed for Unraid servers. It replaces 5+ separate backup tools with one unified, automated solution.[br][br] [b]Core Features:[/b][br] [b]✓ Complete Unraid Backup[/b] — Flash Drive (/boot), Docker Appdata, Virtual Machines, Docker Images, System Configuration[br] [b]✓ Database Dumps[/b] — Auto-detect and safely backup PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, MongoDB databases[br] [b]✓ SHA256 Verification[/b] — Checksum validation for all backup files, detect corruption before restore[br] [b]✓ External Volumes[/b] — Optional per-container backup of data volumes (media, documents, etc.)[br] [b]✓ Retention Policy[/b] — Auto-cleanup old backups (keep minimum + delete older than X days)[br] [b]✓ Pre/Post Hooks[/b] — Run custom scripts before/after backups[br] [b]✓ NAS Power Management[/b] — Wake-on-LAN and automatic shutdown of backup NAS to save energy[br] [b]✓ Multi-Target Support[/b] — Synology NAS, Cloud Storage (B2, S3, Wasabi), External USB/HDD, Remote Servers[br][br] [b]Recovery Mode:[/b][br] [b]✓ Database Restore Wizard[/b] — Step-by-step database restoration with auto-generated commands[br] [b]✓ Verification Before Restore[/b] — Optional checksum verification before recovery[br] [b]✓ One-Click Container Restore[/b] — Generates docker run commands from saved configs[br] [b]✓ Granular File Restore[/b] — Restore individual files or complete directories[br] [b]✓ Flash Drive Restore[/b] — Complete /boot restoration with session-based protection[br] [b]✓ Audit Logging[/b] — Every restore operation is logged for accountability[br][br] [b]Security:[/b][br] [b]✓ First-Run Setup[/b] — No default passwords, create admin account on first access[br] [b]✓ Recovery Key System[/b] — Secure password recovery without backdoors[br] [b]✓ Session-Based Restore Protection[/b] — Multi-layer security for restore operations[br] [b]✓ 3-2-1-1-0 Strategy[/b] — 3 copies, 2 media, 1 offsite, 1 immutable, 0 errors[br][br] [b]User Experience:[/b][br] [b]✓ Futuristic UI[/b] — 4 switchable themes (Cyber Blue, Neon Purple, Matrix Green, Fire Orange)[br] [b]✓ Live Logs[/b] — Real-time backup progress with structured logging[br] [b]✓ i18n[/b] — German and English[br] [b]✓ PWA[/b] — Mobile-optimized, works offline[br][br] [b]First-Time Setup:[/b][br] 1. Install container[br] 2. Access Web UI[br] 3. Create admin account (username + password)[br] 4. SAVE RECOVERY KEY (only shown once!)[br] 5. Start creating backup jobs[br][br] [b]⚠️ IMPORTANT: Write Access Protection[/b][br] This container has write access to /boot and /mnt/user for automated restore capability. All write operations are protected by session-based security - writes only occur during explicit restore operations with multi-step confirmation.[br][br] [b]Documentation:[/b] https://github.com/Kreuzbube88/helbackup

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the HELBACKUP web interface

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Docker socket for container management (stop/start during backups)

Config.Name: Unraid Flash Drive
Config.Description: ⚠️ Unraid USB flash drive (READ-WRITE for automated Flash restore). PROTECTED: Write operations only allowed during explicit restore sessions with multi-step confirmation!

Config.Name: Host System (Restore)
Config.Description: ⚠️ Unraid user array (READ-WRITE for automated restore). PROTECTED: Write operations only allowed during explicit restore sessions with multi-step confirmation!

Config.Name: Libvirt Socket
Config.Description: Libvirt Unix socket - Required for VM stop/start during backups

Config.Name: LIBVIRT_DEFAULT_URI
Config.Description: Libvirt connection URI - do not change unless you know what you are doing

Config.Name: JWT_SECRET
Config.Description: Required: Random secret for authentication. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32

Config.Name: SECURE_COOKIES
Config.Description: Set to false for local HTTP access. Set to true when using HTTPS via reverse proxy.

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Timezone for scheduled backups (e.g., Europe/Berlin, America/New_York)

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Log verbosity: debug, info, warn, error

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file permissions (Unraid default: 99)

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions (Unraid default: 100)
